summary:
The retrieved evidence does not establish a link between exon 38 or 39 KMT2D missense variants and Kabuki syndrome type 1, as no studies identify these specific exons as having pathogenic significance or causal role in disease manifestation. Despite broad knowledge that KMT2D mutations contribute to approximately 75% of KS1 cases, this includes all domains of the gene—not specifically exons 38 or 39.  

\boxed{B}
